title: Avoid False Memories: - Validate Facts: Check with Others Involved or Revisit Records (emails,
subtitle: Reality Check Your Memories
description:
To avoid false memories:
- Validate facts: Check with others involved or revisit records (emails, photos, notes).
- Stay neutral: Avoid over-analyzing or trying too hard to recall—this can create distortions.
- Ask questions: Reflect on whether your memory feels vivid or constructed.

Example: Before insisting that an event happened a certain way, verify it with a trusted source.

faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Quick verifications
howTo.summary: Check one external record like a calendar entry, email, or photo to confirm a memory. Spend no more than 90 seconds on this task to avoid unnecessary disputes.

howTo.title: Neutral questioning
howTo.summary: Ask open-ended questions to others involved in the memory. Frame inquiries collaboratively to avoid leading them and potentially altering their recollection.

howTo.title: Vividness vs. construction
howTo.summary: Use a quick internal checklist to assess the vividness of your memory. If it lacks external validation, treat it as potentially constructed and seek verification.

howTo.title: Document verification norms
howTo.summary: Establish a habit of documenting verifications by adding a one-line note to your records. This practice helps maintain clarity and reduces future disputes.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your verification results in the Brali LifeOS app. Use the link to access the app and track your progress: https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/memory-fact-checker/
